raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command that counts .php files and changes permissions to 755. Probably use find. Count: find /testbed -type f -name '*.php' | wc -l. Change perms: find /testbed -type f -name '*.php' -exec chmod 755 {} +. Combine? Use find twice. One line: find /testbed -type f -name '*.php' -print | tee /dev/stderr | wc -l; find /testbed -type f -name '*.php' -exec chmod 755 {} +. But single line? Use &&. So: find /testbed -type f -name '*.php' -print | tee /dev/stderr | wc -l && find /testbed -type f -name '*.php' -exec chmod 755 {} +. That prints count then changes.
